config._comment_database: Connection to your service's PostgreSQL database.
config._comment_prefix: topic.prefix appears in connector-internal topic names and replication slot names. Use a short, unique identifier for your service. Does NOT affect the final routed topic name.
config._comment_tables: Only watch the outbox table — not your main application tables.
config._comment_replication: plugin.name: use 'pgoutput' (built into Postgres 10+). slot.name: unique replication slot name per connector. publication.name: must match the slot.
config.plugin.name: pgoutput
config.slot.name: debezium_my_service_outbox
config.publication.name: dbz_publication_my_service_outbox
config._comment_snapshot: never: only capture ongoing changes. Your outbox is ephemeral (INSERT+DELETE), so snapshotting initial rows would produce duplicates. Use 'never' unless you have a specific reason not to.
config._comment_transforms: The EventRouter SMT is Debezium's built-in outbox pattern transform. It reads aggregatetype from the INSERT row and routes to the appropriate Kafka topic.
config._comment_field_mapping: Map EventRouter fields to your outbox table columns. These must match your DDL column names.
config._comment_routing: route.by.field: the column whose value becomes the topic suffix. route.topic.replacement: the final topic name pattern. ${routedByValue} is replaced with the aggregatetype column value. Example: aggregatetype='widgets' → topic 'outbox.event.my-service.widgets'
config._comment_payload: expand.json.payload: parse the payload column as JSON rather than emitting it as a raw string. Required if payload is JSONB and the consumer expects a JSON object.
config._comment_headers: Additional columns to forward as Kafka message headers. 'operation' lets the consumer distinguish creates from deletes without parsing the payload. 'version' lets the consumer handle schema evolution.
config._comment_performance: Lower poll interval for lower latency (at the cost of slightly more database load). 500ms is a good starting point for dev/stage; adjust for production.
